title: OpenClaw main lane should fail over faster on transport errors

source.license_note: Synthetic ACBench ops reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62848.

task.summary: Triage slow main-lane failover on repeated provider transport failures.
task.instructions: Use the lane incident evidence to identify why failover happens too late and propose an ops-level repair plan that restores responsiveness.
task.acceptance_notes: This GitHub-derived ops scenario is synthetic and graded behaviorally.

visible_context.issue_text: Repeated transport errors in the main session lane visibly spam connection errors for more than 20 seconds before fallback finally succeeds.
error_logs: embedded run agent end: provider=arcee error=network connection error
embedded run failover decision: decision=fallback_model reason=timeout after ~23s
lane task error: lane=main durationMs=23552 error=FailoverError
reproduction_steps: Inspect the incident notes and repeated retry evidence.
Explain where the failover policy should change and what immediate mitigation restores responsiveness.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models the incident response around a fail-fast and fallback policy problem.

ops_fault.description: Synthetic transport-failure incident for the main session lane derived from the GitHub issue.
